title: Concat: Merge Three Arrays
description: Given three arrays, merge them into a single new array in the order they were provided using `.concat()`.
whatShouldHappen: Pass the second and third arrays as arguments to .concat().
patternExplanation: .concat() is a non-mutating method that combines two or more arrays into a new one.

title: Includes: Word Search
description: Given an array of strings and a target word, return `true` if the word is in the array (exact match), and `false` otherwise.
whatShouldHappen: Pass the target word as the argument.
patternExplanation: .includes() is the modern, readable way to check if an element exists in an array.

title: IndexOf: Check Start and End
description: Given an array and a value, return `true` if the value is located at either the very first position OR the very last position. Otherwise, return `false`.
whatShouldHappen: Check if .lastIndexOf(value) is equal to array length - 1 (last position).
Return true if either condition is true.
patternExplanation: indexOf searches from the start, while lastIndexOf searches from the end. They return -1 if the value is not found.

title: Join: Create CSV
description: Given an array of values, return a string where each value is separated by a comma and a space (e.g., `[1, 2]` becomes `'1, 2'`).
whatShouldHappen: Pass the string ', ' (comma + space) as the separator.
patternExplanation: .join() converts an array into a string by concatenating all elements with a specified separator.
